children.name: Cyanolimnas
children.description: Cyanolimnas — a genus of rails & coots in the family Rallidae.

children.name: Cyanolimnas cerverai
children.description: The Zapata rail is a medium-sized, dark-coloured rail. It has brown upperparts, greyish-blue underparts, a red-based yellow bill, white undertail coverts, and red eyes and legs. Its short wings render it almost flightless.

children.name: Eulabeornis
children.description: Eulabeornis — a genus of rails & coots in the family Rallidae.

children.name: Eulabeornis castaneoventris
children.description: The chestnut rail is a species of bird in the family Rallidae. It is found in the Aru Islands and northern Australia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical mangrove forests.

children.name: Fulica
children.description: Fulica — a genus of rails & coots in the family Rallidae.

children.name: Fulica alai
children.description: The ʻAlae keʻokeʻo, also known as the Hawaiian coot in English, is a bird in the rail family, Rallidae, that is endemic to Hawaii. In Hawaiian, ʻalae is a noun and means mud hen. Keʻo is an adjective for clear or white.

children.name: Fulica americana
children.description: Fulica americana — a species in the genus Fulica, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Fulica ardesiaca
children.description: The Andean coot, also known as the slate-colored coot, is a species of bird in subfamily Rallinae of family Rallidae, the rails, gallinules, and coots. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.

children.name: Fulica armillata
children.description: The red-gartered coot is a species of bird in subfamily Rallinae of family Rallidae, the rails, gallinules, and coots. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Paraguay, and Uruguay.

children.name: Fulica atra
children.description: The Eurasian coot, also known as the common coot or Australian coot, is a member of the rail and crake bird family, the Rallidae. It is found in Europe, Asia, Australia, New Zealand and parts of North Africa. It has a slaty-black body, a glossy black head and a white bill with a white frontal shield.

children.name: Fulica caribaea
children.description: Fulica caribaea — a species in the genus Fulica, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Fulica chathamensis
children.description: Fulica chathamensis — a species in the genus Fulica, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Fulica cornuta
children.description: The horned coot is a species of bird found in the Andes of South America. It was described by Bonaparte in 1853 based on a specimen collected in Bolivia. For a long time it was known only from the type specimen.

children.name: Fulica cristata
children.description: The red-knobbed coot or crested coot is a member of the rail and crake bird family, the Rallidae.

children.name: Fulica gigantea
children.description: Fulica gigantea — a species in the genus Fulica, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Fulica infelix
children.description: Fulica infelix — a species in the genus Fulica, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Fulica leucoptera
children.description: The white-winged coot is a species of bird in the subfamily Rallinae of the family Rallidae. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Paraguay, Uruguay, and the Falkland Islands.

children.name: Fulica newtonii
children.description: Fulica newtonii — a species in the genus Fulica, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Fulica prisca

children.name: Fulica rufifrons
children.description: The red-fronted coot is a species of bird in subfamily Rallinae of family Rallidae, the rails, gallinules, and coots. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Peru, and Uruguay.

children.name: Gallirallus
children.description: Gallirallus — a genus of rails & coots in the family Rallidae.

children.name: Gallirallus australis
children.description: The weka, also known as the Māori hen or woodhen is a flightless bird species of the rail family. It is endemic to New Zealand. Some authorities consider it as the only extant member of the genus Gallirallus.

children.name: Gallirallus calayanensis
children.description: The Calayan rail is a flightless bird of the rail, moorhen, and coot family (Rallidae) that inhabits Calayan Island in the Philippines. Though well known to natives of the island as the "piding", it was first observed by ornithologist Carmela Española in May 2004 and the discovery was officially announced on August 16, 2004. It was formerly the only species placed in the genus Aptenorallus.

children.name: Gallirallus dieffenbachii
children.description: Dieffenbach's rail, known in the Moriori language as meriki or mehoriki, is an extinct flightless bird in the rail family. It was endemic to the Chatham Islands. The species was formerly placed in the genus Hypotaenidia.

children.name: Gallirallus epulare
children.description: Gallirallus epulare — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus ernstmayri
children.description: Gallirallus ernstmayri — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus gracilitibia
children.description: Gallirallus gracilitibia — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus huiatua
children.description: Gallirallus huiatua — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus insignis
children.description: The pink-legged rail, also known as the New Britain rail, is a species of bird in the family Rallidae. The species was formerly placed in the genus Hypotaenidia.

children.name: Gallirallus lafresnayanus
children.description: Gallirallus lafresnayanus — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus modestus
children.description: The Chatham Islands rail, also known as the Chatham rail, is an extinct flightless species of bird in the family Rallidae. It was endemic to Chatham, Mangere and Pitt Islands, in the Chatham archipelago of New Zealand. The Chatham Islands rail was first discovered on Mangere in 1871, and 26 specimens collected there are known from museum collections.

children.name: Gallirallus okinawae
children.description: The Okinawa rail is a species of bird in the rail family, Rallidae. It is endemic to Okinawa Island in Japan where it is known as the Yanbaru kuina . Its existence was only confirmed in 1978 and it was formally described in 1981 although unidentified rails had been recorded on the island since at least 1973 and local stories of a bird known as the agachi kumira may refer to this species.

children.name: Gallirallus owstoni
children.description: Gallirallus owstoni — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus pacificus
children.description: The Tahiti rail, Tahitian red-billed rail, or Pacific red-billed rail is an extinct species of rail that lived on Tahiti. It was first recorded during James Cook's second voyage around the world (1772–1775), on which it was illustrated by Georg Forster and described by Johann Reinhold Forster. No specimens have been preserved.

children.name: Gallirallus pendiculentus
children.description: Gallirallus pendiculentus — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus philippensis
children.description: Gallirallus philippensis — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus pisonii
children.description: Gallirallus pisonii — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus poecilopterus
children.description: Gallirallus poecilopterus — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus ripleyi
children.description: Gallirallus ripleyi — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus rovianae
children.description: The Roviana rail is a species of bird in the family Rallidae. It is endemic to the Western Province. The species has sometimes been placed in the genus Hypotaenidia.

children.name: Gallirallus sharpei
children.description: Gallirallus sharpei — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus storrsolsoni
children.description: Gallirallus storrsolsoni — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus striatus
children.description: The slaty-breasted rail is a rail species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. Breeding has been recorded in July near Dehradun in the foothills of the Indian Himalayas. Despite traditionally being considered part of Gallirallus, recent genetic studies have consistently placed it in the genus Lewinia, which is now formally recognised by the IUCN and IOC.

children.name: Gallirallus sylvestris
children.description: Gallirallus sylvestris — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus temptatus
children.description: Gallirallus temptatus — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus torquatus
children.description: Gallirallus torquatus — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus vekamatolu
children.description: Gallirallus vekamatolu — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Gallirallus wakensis
children.description: The extinct Wake Island rail or Wake rail is an extinct flightless rail and the only native land bird on the Pacific atoll of Wake. It was found on the islands of Wake and Wilkes, and Peale, which is separated from the others by a channel of about 100 metres (330 ft). It was hunted to extinction during World War II.

children.name: Gallirallus woodfordi
children.description: Gallirallus woodfordi — a species in the genus Gallirallus, family Rallidae. Also known as rails & coots.

children.name: Rallus aquaticus
children.description: The water rail, western water rail or European water rail is a bird of the rail family which breeds in well-vegetated wetlands across Europe, Asia and North Africa. Northern and eastern populations are migratory, but this species is a permanent resident in the warmer parts of its breeding range. The adult is 23–28 cm (9–11 in) long, and, like other rails, has a body that is flattened laterally, allowing it easier passage through the reed beds it inhabits.
